title: MongoDB Q3 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
text: MongoDB Q3 2021 earnings showed total revenue of $150.8 million, up 38% year-over-year. Subscription revenue reached $144.1 million, up 39% YoY, with Atlas revenue growing 61% YoY and now representing 47% of total revenue. Total customers exceeded 22,600, up from 15,900 year-ago, while Atlas customers surpassed 21,100 compared to 14,200 previously. Customers with $100,000+ ARR reached 898, up from 688. Non-GAAP operating loss was $16 million with -11% margin. Net loss per share was $0.31 on 59.4 million weighted shares. CEO Dev Ittycheria stated we saw another record quarter of customer additions both in direct sales and self-serve channels. MongoDB leadership emphasized three competitive advantages: superior database technology, strong developer community adoption, and multi-cloud platform independence capabilities. FY2021 guidance was raised to Revenue $574.4-$576.4 million with Non-GAAP net loss per share $1.07-$1.04. The company expects continued COVID-19 pandemic impacts into Q4.
